description: Group identical self-text → TWO rows (hermetic, single-instance): the group analog of l3_identical_self_text (Bug-F). Send the SAME byte-identical text into a group twice; both must persist as two distinct rows. Group self-sends are isSelf=true and carry unique sequenced msgIDs, so the incoming-only content-dedup in MessageHistoryPersistence.appendHistory must NOT collapse them. equals:2 proves no false content-dedup of self group-sends. The leading isConnected==true gate forces the CONNECTED direct-append path (codex P1) — the offline-queue path would also append local rows, so connection is asserted up front; the duplicate text precludes a per-row isPending assertion here, so the gate carries that guarantee. Asserts on the LIVE group via conv='group_{{gid}}'; leave deferred to teardown (quitGroup wipes group history).
